prompt.text:
Build a lightweight but COMPLETE Sales CRM in NocoBase — FULL AUTONOMY, no questions, end to end. Build into env **{ENV}** — every `nb api` call passes `-e {ENV} -y`. The instance is a CLEAN empty NocoBase (beta-full, commercial plugins available).

First read the `nocobase-prototype-repro` skill (SKILL.md + references/) and `nocobase-app-discipline`.

REFERENCE PROTOTYPE (match its layout + signature pages): http://localhost:8080/205-crm-pipeline.html
It embeds an authoritative `<script type="application/nb-spec+json">` spec (collections, pages, workflows, roles, users, aiEmployee, notes). HONOR that spec — it is the target.

Deliver ALL FIVE layers, not just pages:

1) DATA MODEL — snake_case collections; relation fields named as NOUNS, never `*_id` (beta rejects `*_id` association names):
- crm_customers: name, industry (enum: SaaS/Manufacturing/Retail/Finance/Healthcare), tier (enum: A/B/C), status (enum: prospect/active/churned), website, phone, address (text), owner (belongsTo users)
- crm_contacts: name, title, email, phone, is_primary (checkbox), customer (belongsTo crm_customers)
- crm_deals: title, stage (enum: lead/qualified/proposal/negotiation/won/lost), amount (number, 2 decimals), close_date (date), probability (number 0-100), source (enum: inbound/outbound/referral/event), customer (belongsTo crm_customers), owner (belongsTo users)
- crm_activities: subject, type (enum: call/email/meeting/task), due_date (date), status (enum: open/done), notes (text), deal (belongsTo crm_deals), customer (belongsTo crm_customers), owner (belongsTo users)
SEED all-English data: >=12 customers (cover every industry/tier/status), >=14 contacts, >=16 deals across EVERY stage (incl. won & lost), >=16 activities across every type with a few due today.

2) PAGES (native-first; honor the prototype's region->block map) under a top-level "CRM" menu group:
- Dashboard: native Chart blocks (deals by stage funnel/bar, pipeline amount, deals by owner) + a KPI strip + a "Closing soon" table + a recent-activities list.
- Pipeline: Kanban of crm_deals grouped by `stage` (card shows title + customer + amount — kanban cards allow <=2 native fields, so add a JS card field for the rich card) + a Table view with Filter + Add.
- Customers: Table + Filter + Add; row click -> a WIDE drawer with customer Details + three sub-tables (Contacts / Deals / Activities), each auto-filtered to the parent via `associationName` + `sourceId={{ctx.view.inputArgs.filterByTk}}`.
- Contacts: Table + Filter.
- Activities: Calendar (by due_date) + Table; filter by type and status.

3) WORKFLOWS (create AND enable via the workflow API):
- "Lead intake": collection trigger on crm_deals create where stage=lead -> default probability=10 + create a crm_activities row (type=call, subject "First call", owner = the deal's owner, due_date = +2 days).
- "Deal won": collection trigger on crm_deals update stage->won -> set the customer's status=active + create an onboarding activity.
- "High-value approval": when a crm_deals amount >= 50000 -> an approval workflow; a sales_manager must approve before the deal advances to negotiation/won.

4) ROLES / ACL (nb roles + data-permission scopes):
- sales_rep: CRUD crm_* but scoped to records where owner = current user (data scope on owner.id = {{ctx.user.id}}); read others; CANNOT approve.
- sales_manager: full crm_* + approve deals + full dashboard.
- admin: full access.
Seed USERS and assign as owners across the seed data: Admin User (admin/root), Morgan Lee (sales_manager), Alex Rivera + Priya Shah (sales_rep).

5) AI EMPLOYEE "Sales Assistant" — configure it on the PRE-PROVISIONED LLM service. An OpenCode Zen gateway key is ALREADY configured on this instance as an `llmService` with `enabledModels`; USE it (do NOT create keys). Skills (pass as string arrays): (a) "Summarize customer" — reads a customer + its deals/activities and returns a briefing; (b) "Draft follow-up email" — formFiller drafts into an activity note; (c) "Score lead" — fills crm_deals.probability + a short rationale. Surface it as an AIEmployeeButtonModel on the Customer detail and the Deal form.

HONOR these NocoBase gotchas: belongsTo relations are NOUNS not `*_id`; drawer sub-tables use `associationName` + `sourceId={{ctx.view.inputArgs.filterByTk}}`; kanban needs a JS card field; every select->Tag mapping falls back to the raw value for unknown enums; seed every enum branch; never `flowModels:update` (clears parentId).

VERIFY before done: open each page in a fresh no-cache browser (inject `localStorage.NOCOBASE_TOKEN`), confirm every block renders with no console/page errors, and do a visual pass vs the prototype.

END with a FINAL REPORT: collections built; the signature page schemaUids (Dashboard + Pipeline + Customers); workflows created + enabled; roles + a one-line permission matrix; the AI employee id; `Self-Score: N` (0-10); and a short list of gaps/tradeoffs.

Base directory for this skill: ~/.claude/skills/nocobase-prototype-repro

# Goal

Turn a prototype + a one-line ask into a faithful NocoBase app. This is a **meta / orchestration skill** — it sequences `nocobase-data-modeling` + `nocobase-ui-builder` + `nocobase-app-discipline` and adds the two things that decide fidelity: a **region → native-block mapping** and a **visual convergence loop**. It does not re-teach the CLI; it routes to the sibling skills for mechanics and owns only the reproduction-specific judgment.

# When to use / Non-Goals

**Hard gate (check before selecting this skill):** there must be an actual prototype in hand — an HTML file, a screenshot, or a link to one — that you are matching against. No prototype, no fidelity gap → this is **not** the skill; a plain "build me an app / a page" request belongs to `nocobase-app-discipline` (whole system) or `nocobase-ui-builder` (single page/edit). Do not let this skill shadow the normal build flow.

**Use when:**
- The user gives a prototype (HTML file, link, screenshot, or a `public/prototypes/...` URL to curl) and wants it reproduced in NocoBase.
- A page already exists but the user says it's monotone / ugly / doesn't match the prototype / a region should be a list/card/kanban instead of a table — i.e. there is a **visual target and a fidelity gap**.

**Non-Goals — hand off, do not do here:**
- Pure data modeling (collections/fields/relations with no visual target) → `nocobase-data-modeling`.
- Workflows / automations → `nocobase-workflow-manage`. ACL / permissions → `nocobase-acl-manage`. Plugin work → `nocobase-plugin-development`.
- A localized UI edit with **no visual target and no fidelity gap** (move one field, rename an action, tweak a reaction) → `nocobase-ui-builder` **directly**. This skill is only worth its overhead when you are matching a prototype's look/behavior; for a mechanical surface edit, skip straight to ui-builder.
- Whole-page authoring from a business sentence with **no** prototype → author a quick prototype first ([prototype-authoring.md](references/prototype-authoring.md)), then this skill; or if the user just wants a generic page, that's `nocobase-ui-builder`'s `applyBlueprint`.

# Router — task shape → read first

Hit one route, read its "first" doc, and only open the "then" docs when that route is still not enough. Sibling skills own the mechanics; the reference files here own the reproduction-specific judgment.

| Task shape | Read first | Then, as needed |
|---|---|---|
| Prototype handed over → reproduce end-to-end | [spec-template.md](references/spec-template.md) (Phase 1 SPEC) | [decomposition-grammar.md](references/decomposition-grammar.md), [block-recipes.md](references/block-recipes.md), [gotchas.md](references/gotchas.md), [build-discipline.md](references/build-discipline.md), [visual-loop.md](references/visual-loop.md) |
| Build/verify loop — write→verify→E2E gates, "is it correct/proven", or a build breaks for one role only | [build-discipline.md](references/build-discipline.md) | [gotchas.md](references/gotchas.md); `scripts/nb-page-verify.py` (multi-account render/error gate) |

| "Which block for this region?" | the **Region → native block** table below | [decomposition-grammar.md](references/decomposition-grammar.md), [block-recipes.md](references/block-recipes.md) |

transcript.text:
Base directory for this skill: ~/.claude/skills/nocobase-app-discipline

# NocoBase App Discipline

## When to use

Invoke this skill at the very start of any task that says "build me a [business] system" / "搭一个 X 系统" / "create a CRM/HRM/PM/inventory/helpdesk app". This is a **meta skill** — it shapes how the other `nocobase-*` skills are used; it does NOT replace them.

**Do NOT invoke** for single-point edits (add one field / rename one page / tweak one action) — go directly to `nocobase-ui-builder` for those.

> **⚠️ Prototype handed over? Don't stop at native tables.** If the request includes a prototype to match — an HTML/image/link, or any "match the layout / signature visuals of this prototype: \<url\>" — then the **signature page(s) MUST be built through [`nocobase-prototype-repro`](../nocobase-prototype-repro/SKILL.md)** (analyze prototype → region→native-block map → screenshot-vs-prototype visual loop), **not** as a generic list/table page. This skill still owns the overall 6-phase flow, data model, seed, and the plain CRUD pages; but the moment a prototype exists, hand each signature region to prototype-repro. The "build me an X app" wording does **not** cancel the prototype — a prompt can be both. Skipping this is the #1 cause of a faithful-looking prototype collapsing into three stacked tables.

## Reference systems — empirical comparison (read this FIRST)

> Comparison of 4 systems we built end-to-end. **Follow the ✅ patterns, avoid the two ⚠️ pitfalls.**

### ✅ Learn from these

| System | Time | Output | Key correct decisions |
|---|---|---|---|
| **CRM** | 16min | 5 collections / 5 pages / sub-tables in drawer | **Single-line relations + sub-tables use `associationName + sourceId={{ctx.view.inputArgs.filterByTk}}` + zero scope creep** |
| **HRM** | 25min | 4 collections (incl. `departments` as tree) / 4 pages / bidirectional hasMany / approval buttons | **`departments` uses tree template; `employees.manager_id` self-reference auto-creates `subordinates`; approvals use `UpdateRecordActionModel` to flip status, not custom code** |

### ⚠️ Avoid these anti-patterns

| System | Time | Problem | Lesson |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Anthropic PM** | 12min | Original prompt asked for 4 models — agent added 4 extra tables and 4 pages (Sprints / Tags / Dashboard / My Tasks); `task_files` table was created but left empty | **Scope creep** — when boundaries are ambiguous, agent adds features. **Read** [scope-discipline.md](references/scope-discipline.md) |
| **DS PM** | 28min | "Project detail page" interpreted literally → standalone route page that cannot auto-filter by current project (no `ctx.view.inputArgs.filterByTk` context); prepare-write iterated repeatedly on multi-tab + nested popup | **Literal interpretation** — "details" should be a drawer. **Read** [word-mapping.md](references/word-mapping.md) |

## 6-phase mandatory order

| Phase | Goal | Checkpoint to enter next |
|-------|------|--------------------|
| **1. Lock-in** | Parse prompt → list collections/fields/pages count + business template routing + word-mapping | User confirms task list |
| **2. Data modeling** | Build all collections + fields + relations in one pass; **never edit while building** | `fields list` confirms relation count matches |
| **3. Lightweight seed** | Pre-fill main tables with 1-2 rows for phase-4 UI rendering | Each main table has ≥ 1 row |
| **4. Master list pages** | One page per collection (Filter + AddNew + Table + ViewAction) | `flow-surfaces get` validates page schema |
| **5. Detail drawer + sub-tables** | ViewAction opens drawer with DetailsBlock + sub-table TableBlocks (`associationName + sourceId`) | Drawer sub-tables auto-filter by parent id |
| **6. Full seed + final report** | Seed per [seed-data-standards.md](references/seed-data-standards.md) + 4-class verification + report | User responds (end of task) |

Details in [build-order.md](references/build-order.md).

## 8 hard constraints

1. **H1**: Never auto-add

# Goal

Use the available NocoBase data-modeling surface to inspect and change collections, fields, relations, and view-backed schemas.

Prefer the transport in this order:

- the `nb api data-modeling` CLI whenever it is available
- another equivalent data-modeling transport only when the CLI is unavailable and it exposes the same operation surface

Do not make the skill depend on one executable name. Treat CLI command names and equivalent wrappers as transport details around the same modeling operations.

Transport-selection rule:

1. Check whether the `nb api data-modeling` CLI is available in the current environment.
2. If it is available, use the CLI.
3. If the CLI is available but not authenticated for the target app, stop and guide the user to authenticate the CLI.
4. Only fall back to another equivalent transport when the CLI itself is unavailable.

Read `references/decision-matrix.md` first when the request is broad or the correct modeling path is unclear.

# Mandatory Gates

1. Confirm the chosen data-modeling transport is reachable and authenticated before any write operation. If `nb api data-modeling` CLI is available, that should be the chosen transport.
2. For plugin-backed tables or fields, read `references/plugin-provided-capabilities.md` before mutating schema.
3. For `view` collections, verify the upstream database view exists with `db-views list|get` before creating or updating anything.
4. Before using a CLI modeling command you have not used yet in the current task, run its `--help` once and follow the generated help text for flags and examples. When CLI is unavailable and another non-CLI transport must be used, inspect its exposed parameter schema before first use.

Stop and ask the user to fix auth when the chosen transport returns `401`, `403`, `Auth required`, or equivalent access errors. If the chosen transport is `nb api data-modeling` CLI, guide the user to restore CLI authentication rather than switching transports.

# Final Command Surface

Use only this final data-modeling operation surface:

- Inspect collections: `collections list`
- Inspect one collection: `collections get`
- Inspect fields in one collection: `collections fields list`
- Create or update a collection with compact payload: `collections apply`
- Create or update fields with compact payload: `fields apply`
- Delete a collection: `collections destroy`
- Inspect database views: `db-views list|get`

When the transport is CLI-based, prefer learning exact flags from help instead of keeping large command-shape reminders in prompt context:

- `nb api data-modeling collections apply --help`
- `nb api data-modeling collections fields list --help`
- `nb api data-modeling fields apply --help`
- `nb api data-modeling collections destroy --help`

Do not prefer older low-level collection or nested field commands when the final command surface can handle the task.

# Core Rules

1. Decide collection type first. Never infer `general`, `tree`, `file`, `calendar`, `sql`, `view`, or `inherit` from the name alone.
2. If the user explicitly asks for a file table, file collection, `template: "file"`, or uses wording such as "文件表", "合同文件", "扫描件", "证书文件", or "the file itself is the record", treat that as a binding collection-type requirement and choose `template: "file"` first.
3. Do not reinterpret a file-first request as a `general` collection just because extra metadata fields are also needed.
4. Prefer the compact payloads supported by `collections apply` and `fields apply`. Let the server fill derived defaults.
5. Do not guess special capabilities. Check references first for plugin-backed fields, relation variants, special collection types, and view-backed models.
6. Relations come after the base collection and scalar fields are correct.

transcript.text:
Base directory for this skill: ~/.claude/skills/nocobase-acl-manage

# Goal

Turn ACL and permission governance into a task-driven workflow so users can ask for business outcomes while the skill handles:

- CLI command selection and argument shaping
- capability checks and safety guards
- write confirmation and readback evidence
- risk-oriented explanation for high-impact changes

# Scope

- This skill is CLI-first for reads and writes.
- Tasks are grouped into five domains:
- role
- global role mode
- permission
- user
- risk assessment
- Every write task follows `plan -> confirm -> apply -> readback`.
- `global role mode` is treated as a global system policy, not a per-role field.

# Non-Goals

- Do not bypass CLI by calling direct REST endpoints.
- Do not mutate ACL through ad-hoc database operations.
- Do not create temporary script files to execute ACL writes.
- Do not invoke other skills for env/plugin bootstrap; use direct `nb` commands in this skill.
- Do not hide high-impact blast radius (global mode, broad snippets, broad strategy actions).
- Do not claim one-click coverage for workflows that require governance review.

# Canonical Task Model

## A) Role Domain

| Task | User Outcome | Required Inputs | Optional Inputs |
|---|---|---|---|
| `role.audit-all` | list all roles with comparable policy summary | none | `data_source_key`, `output` |
| `role.create-blank` | create a role with default read-only baseline (single creation mode) | `role_name` | `role_title`, `description`, `hidden`, `allow_configure`, `allow_new_menu` |
| `role.compare` | explain differences between roles | `role_names[]` | `data_source_key`, `output` |

## B) Global Role-Mode Domain

| Task | User Outcome | Required Inputs | Optional Inputs |
|---|---|---|---|
| `global.role-mode.get` | read current global role mode | none | `output` |
| `global.role-mode.set` | switch global role mode | `role_mode` | `strict_mode` |

`role_mode` enum mapping:

- `default`: independent role usage (no union mode)
- `allow-use-union`: union mode available, role switching still allowed
- `only-use-union`: force union mode for multi-role users

## C) Permission Domain

| Task | User Outcome | Required Inputs | Optional Inputs |
|---|---|---|---|
| `permission.system-snippets.set` | set role-level system snippets | `role_name`, (`snippet_preset` or `snippets`) | none |
| `permission.route.desktop.set` | set desktop route permissions for a role | `role_name`, `route_ids[]` | `set_mode` (`set` or `add` or `remove`) |
| `permission.data-source.global.set` | set global strategy actions for all tables in one data source | `role_name`, `global_actions[]` | `data_source_key` |
| `permission.data-source.resource.set` | set independent actions for one or more collections | `role_name`, (`collection_hint` or `collection_hints[]`), `actions[]` | `data_source_key`, `fields_map`, `scope_map`, `resource_scope` (`all` by default) |
| `permission.scope.manage` | create/update/list reusable scopes | `scope_task` | `data_source_key`, `scope_id`, `scope_payload` |

## D) User Domain

| Task | User Outcome | Required Inputs | Optional Inputs |
|---|---|---|---|
| `user.assign-role` | assign one role to one or many users | `role_name`, (`user_ids[]` or `user_filter`) | `allow_generic_association_write` |
| `user.unassign-role` | remove one role from one or many users | `role_name`, (`user_ids[]` or `user_filter`) | `allow_generic_association_write` |
| `user.audit-role-membership` | inspect users bound to roles | (`role_name` or `user_id`) | `output` |

## E) Risk Domain

| Task | User Outcome | Required Inputs | Optional Inputs |
|---|---|---|---|
| `risk.assess-role` | risk score and rationale for one role | `role_name` | `data_source_key`, `output` |
| `risk.assess-user` | risk score based on user-role-permission relationship | `user_id` | `data_source_key`, `output` |
| `risk.assess-system` | system-level ACL governance risk summary | none | `data_source_key`, `output` |

Base directory for this skill: ~/.claude/skills/nocobase-workflow-manage

# Goal

Use the `nb api workflow` command surface to orchestrate workflows end-to-end: design trigger logic, build node chains, manage versions, and inspect execution results.

CLI usage rules:

1. Use `nb api workflow` as the canonical command namespace for workflow operations.
2. If the CLI is available but not authenticated for the target app, stop and guide the user to authenticate the CLI before continuing.
3. Before using an `nb api workflow` subcommand you have not used yet in the current task, run its `-h` once and follow the generated help text for flags and examples.

# Scope

- Handle: inspect workflow definitions, nodes, version state, executions, and failed jobs.
- Handle: create new workflows for business targets, then configure triggers and nodes sequentially.
- Handle: update existing workflows by creating a new revision first when the current version is frozen.
- Handle: enable workflows, move/duplicate/delete nodes or branches, and execute workflows only after explicit high-risk confirmation.
- Handle: diagnose failed executions and identify the failed job, node, or configuration mismatch.

# Non-Goals

- Do not handle `nb` installation or login bootstrap in this skill.
- Do not design data models, collections, or field schemas from scratch. Hand off to `nocobase-data-modeling`.
- Do not invent filter syntax, evaluator functions, node types, trigger types, collection names, field names, or node keys.
- Do not bypass workflow-specific CLI interfaces with generic CRUD or local source edits.
- Do not delete whole workflows in this skill. If a user explicitly wants workflow deletion, stop and request a separately reviewed path.

## Environment and Tooling

1. `nb` CLI must be available and authenticated to perform workflow operations.

## Clarification and Mutation Preconditions

- Max clarification rounds: `2`
- Max questions per round: `3`
- Default execution bias:
  - If the intended action path is at least `70%` confident and the remaining uncertainty is in fields that can be safely revised after creation, proceed without asking. # FINAL REPORT — SalesFlow CRM on env `fablecrm`

Complete build, all five layers delivered and verified end-to-end at `http://127.0.0.1:14241` (NocoBase 2.2.0-beta.9).

## 1. Collections built (main data source, snake_case, noun relations)

| Collection | Fields | Relations (belongsTo as nouns) |
|---|---|---|
| `crm_customers` | name, industry×5, tier A/B/C, status×3, website, phone, address | `owner`→users; o2m contacts/deals/activities |
| `crm_contacts` | name, title, email, phone, is_primary | `customer`→crm_customers |
| `crm_deals` | title, stage×6, amount (2dp), close_date, probability, source×4 | `customer`, `owner`→users; o2m activities |
| `crm_activities` | subject, type×4, due_date, status open/done, notes | `deal`, `customer`, `owner`→users |

Seed: **12 customers** (all 5 industries, all tiers, all statuses) · **14 contacts** · **16 deals** (3 lead / 3 qualified / 2 proposal / 2 negotiation / 3 won / 3 lost — $365.5k open pipeline, matching the prototype) · **16 activities** (4 of each type, **4 due today**), owners distributed Morgan/Alex/Priya exactly per spec.

## 2. Signature page schemaUids (all 6 under the "CRM" menu group, routeId 374665974972416)

- **Dashboard: `4sq7alvizlj`** — JS KPI strip (live: $365.5k / 50% / 10 open / 4 due today), custom-ECharts stage funnel with per-stage colors + `$·deals` labels, owner bar chart, "Closing soon" table (dataScope: open deals, sorted by close date), "Recent activity" List with JS cards (type icons, status pills, association join)
- **Pipeline: `27j6w8m1gq5`** — native Kanban `groupField=stage` (6 columns) with a rich **JS card field** (customer avatar + title + customer·source + amount + days-to-close chip + owner avatar) + "All deals" table tab with Filter + Add
- **Customers: `ro3mtrcnb4d`** — Table + Filter + Add; View opens a **wide (large) drawer** with Details + 3 sub-tables (Contacts / Deals / Activities) each bound via `associationName` + `sourceId/filterByTk = {{ctx.view.inputArgs.filterByTk}}` — browser-asserted: Northwind drawer shows exactly its 2 contacts / 2 deals / 3 activities, zero leakage
- Contacts `y3f698uy1je` · Activities `wtlnpfnp3ag` (Feed cards / Calendar by due_date / Table, filter by type+status) · Blueprint `7mzs3jpxu5y` (styled system doc)

## 3. Workflows — created AND enabled via the workflow API, probe-verified

| Workflow | id | Probe evidence |
|---|---|---|
| Lead intake (collection, create, stage=lead) | 374668772573184 | probability null→**10**, "First call" call activity created, owner = deal owner, due **+2d** |
| Deal won (collection, update, stage→won) | 374668898402304 | customer status prospect→**active**, "Onboarding kickoff" created |
| High-value approval (approval trigger, ≥50k condition → manager approval; approved→negotiation, rejected→revert) | 374669814857728 (rev of key `xwn3c4mrtgf`) | 60k deal → task assigned to **Morgan (sales_manager)** → approve ⇒ stage=**negotiation**; 70k deal → reject ⇒ stage stays **proposal** |

All probe records deleted afterwards; seed counts restored to exactly 12/14/16/16.

## 4. Roles / ACL (verified matrix)

| Role | view | create | update / destroy | approve | UI routes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `sales_rep` (Alex 3, Priya 4) | all crm_* | yes | **own only** (scope `owner.id = {{ctx.state.currentUser.id}}`; contacts via `customer.owner`) | no | all 16 CRM routes |
| `sales_manager` (Morgan 2) | all | yes | all | **yes** | all 16 CRM routes |
| `admin` (Admin User 1, admin/root) | full system | | | | |

Asserted via API: Alex reads all 16 deals, updates his own, **cannot** update/destroy Priya's (0 rows matched); Morgan updates anyone's. All 6 pages render clean (no console/page errors, no blanks) for **admin + morgan + alex**. Passwords for seed users: `Nb123456!`.

## 5. AI employee

**`sales-assistant`** ("Sales Assistant") on the pre-provisioned `opencode-zen` llmService, model `qwen3.7-max` (from its existing `enabledModels` — no keys created). Skills as string arrays: `
